title: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS)
focus: Definition, components, and features of ADAS technology.
rationale: A standalone technological concept relevant to science and technology applications in daily life.
sectionExcerpt: A built-in suite of coordinated technologies that help drivers operate their vehicles more safely and avoid human errors. In ADAS, sensors such as Lidar, Radar, and cameras work together to inform features such as pedestrian detection, and lane departure warning.
evidenceBullets: ADAS uses sensors like Lidar, Radar, and cameras to improve vehicle safety.
Features include Adaptive Cruise Control, Automatic Emergency Braking, Blind Spot Detection, and Lane Departure Warning.

title: Biostimulants
focus: Definition, regulatory framework, and distinction of biostimulants from fertilizers and pesticides.
rationale: Highly examinable for UPSC Agriculture and Environment sections, particularly its unique definition and regulatory status under the Fertiliser Control Order.
sectionExcerpt: Substances and/or microorganisms whose function when applied to plants or the rhizosphere is to stimulate natural processes to benefit crop quality, independently of its nutrient content. Unlike fertilisers, it does not supply nutrients directly, and unlike pesticides, it does not control pests. They are regulated under Fertiliser (Inorganic, Organic or Mixed) (Control) Order, 1985.
evidenceBullets: Biostimulants stimulate natural processes to benefit crop quality independently of their nutrient content.
They do not supply nutrients directly (unlike fertilizers) and do not control pests (unlike pesticides).
They are regulated under the Fertiliser (Inorganic, Organic or Mixed) (Control) Order, 1985.
Examples include humic acids, seaweed extracts, liquid manure composting, and beneficial bacteria and fungi.

title: Agrienics Programme
focus: Objectives, domain, and nodal agency of the Agrienics Programme.
rationale: A specific government initiative under MeitY with a clear nodal agency, suitable for a factual Prelims question.
sectionExcerpt: A national programme of MeitY involving research, development, deployment, demonstration, and commercialization of technologies in agriculture and environment domain. Nodal Agency: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), Kolkata.
evidenceBullets: The Agrienics Programme is a national initiative of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
It focuses on technology commercialization and deployment in the agriculture and environment domains.
The Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), Kolkata is the Nodal Agency.

title: Metre Convention, 1875
focus: Purpose of the convention, the institution it created (BIPM), and India's membership status.
rationale: Historical international treaty with contemporary relevance due to its 150th anniversary; tests knowledge of international bodies and India's participation.
sectionExcerpt: Includes convention and its annexed regulation to assure the international unification and improvement of the metric system. Created the International Bureau of Weights and Measures (BIPM) to develop and maintain a universal measurement system. India is a member.
evidenceBullets: The Metre Convention of 1875 aims to assure the international unification and improvement of the metric system.
It established the International Bureau of Weights and Measures (BIPM).
India is a member of the Metre Convention.
